ops.title: Repo-local tool helpers factor into tools/fleetlib, never stay inline in one-off scripts; fleet membership is discovered, never hard-coded
ops.body:
When a cc-skills tool script grows general-purpose helpers (subprocess run wrappers, gh fleet discovery, 1Password ops, deploy-key/secret installers), factor them into `tools/fleetlib` — a repo-local uv_build package (gd-build-shaped) that PEP 723 scripts consume — instead of keeping them inline. Shipped 519262e (2026-07-20): `fleetlib.proc/gh/op`, with `gh.repos_with_file(path)` as the canonical fleet-discovery primitive.

**Why:** User rejected a single-file fix with "these are all helpers that sound like they would be useful across cc-skills, think about making this a cc-skills-wide set of utilities." Related earlier correction: hard-coding a repo list is "entirely the model we moved away from" — fleet membership is self-defining (e.g. presence of `.github/workflows/guides.yml`), see [[fleet-scope-means-all-active-repos]].

**How to apply:** New fleet-ops tools import from fleetlib (`sys.path` bridge today; `[tool.uv.sources]` path dep once cc-notes task f3e14ea lands the capt-hook PEP 723 fence carve-out — the verbose-comment hook currently blocks fences >3 lines). Scope stays repo-local: plugin scripts ship via the marketplace cache and stay self-contained. Never enumerate fleet repos statically — discover via `gh.repos_with_file`/`gh.active_repos`.
